questions.question: Explain the concept of virtual memory and how it creates an illusion of larger memory for processes.
keyPoints: Virtual memory provides logical address space larger than physical memory
Uses disk as backing store for pages not in RAM
OS maintains mapping between virtual and physical addresses
Enables programs to execute without being aware of physical memory limitations

questions.question: What is demand paging? Explain its advantages and disadvantages.
keyPoints: Pages loaded only when referenced (on page fault)
Initial memory footprint is small
I/O is deferred until actually needed
Disadvantage: initial page faults cause delay
Reduces unnecessary memory allocation

questions.question: Define thrashing and explain the working set model as a solution.
keyPoints: Thrashing: excessive paging I/O degrading CPU performance
Working set: pages actively used in recent time interval τ
Goal: maintain enough frames for process working set
If working set > frames, process thrashes
Solution: admission control and suspending processes

questions.question: Explain the FIFO page replacement algorithm with an example showing Belady's anomaly.
keyPoints: FIFO maintains pages in arrival order (queue)
Evicts oldest page when memory is full
Simple to implement with circular buffer
Belady's anomaly: more frames can cause more page faults
Example: reference string 1,2,3,4,1,2,5,1,2,3,4,5 with 3 vs 4 frames

questions.question: Describe the structure of a page table entry and the purpose of each field.
keyPoints: Frame number: physical location of page
Valid/Invalid bit: page resident in memory
Dirty/Modified bit: page modified since loaded
Reference/Use bit: for replacement algorithms
Protection bits: read/write/execute permissions
Optional: sharing information

questions.question: Explain the Copy-on-Write mechanism used in modern operating systems.
keyPoints: Parent and child processes share pages initially
Pages marked as read-only
On write attempt, OS creates private copy
Subsequent modifications use separate copies
Reduces memory overhead during fork() operations

questions.question: What is the Translation Lookaside Buffer (TLB)? Explain its role in virtual memory.
keyPoints: Hardware cache for address translation
Stores recent virtual-to-physical mappings
Accessed in parallel with page table lookup
Hit: direct physical address generation
Miss: page table lookup and TLB update
Improves effective memory access time

questions.question: Explain the following page replacement algorithms with an example for each: FIFO, LRU, and Optimal. Show why Belady's anomaly occurs in FIFO.
keyPoints: FIFO: Queue-based eviction, oldest page replaced
LRU: Evicts least recently used page, needs hardware support
Optimal: Theoretical algorithm, replaces page used farthest in future
Example with reference string to demonstrate each
Belady's anomaly: counter-intuitive behavior in FIFO
Explain why FIFO suffers from this: doesn't consider page usage patterns

questions.question: Explain the page fault handling mechanism in detail. Also calculate effective access time assuming page fault service time is 8ms and memory access time is 100ns with a page fault rate of 0.001.
keyPoints: Page fault handling steps in sequence
Need for free frame selection and disk I/O
Effective access time = (1-p) × Ma + p × Pf
Calculation: 0.999 × 100ns + 0.001 × 8ms
Result: approximately 8 microseconds
Explain impact of page faults on performance

questions.question: Describe different page table structures: hierarchical, hashed, and inverted page tables. Discuss their advantages and disadvantages.
keyPoints: Hierarchical: multi-level page tables for large address spaces
Hashed: uses hash table for faster lookup, good for large pages
Inverted: one entry per physical frame, reduces memory
Advantages: memory efficiency, fast lookup
Disadvantages: longer lookup time, sharing complexity
